items.question: In which year did the World Heritage framework add a category for cultural landscapes?
items.explanation: The UNESCO convention timeline notes cultural landscapes were added in 1992.

items.question: Properties considered for the World Heritage List must demonstrate what kind of value?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention overview centers on outstanding universal value for listed properties.

items.question: Sustainable Development Goal target 11.4 calls for strengthened efforts to protect and safeguard what?
items.correctAnswer: the world’s cultural and natural heritage
items.explanation: UN SDG 11 targets list 11.4 on protecting cultural and natural heritage.

items.question: The Budapest Declaration on World Heritage, expanding strategic objectives, was adopted during which anniversary of the Convention?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline links the Budapest Declaration to the Convention’s 30th anniversary in 2002.

items.question: The Nara Document on Authenticity, cited in UNESCO’s World Heritage strategy timeline, was adopted in which year?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline associates the Nara Document with 1994 Global Strategy work.

items.question: The UNESCO World Heritage Convention linking nature conservation and cultural property protection was adopted in which year?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention timeline dates adoption to 1972.

items.question: The World Heritage Convention was adopted by the UNESCO General Conference on which date in 1972?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline records adoption on 16 November 1972.

items.question: The approach widely known as cultural geography in the United States was especially associated with which geographer’s Berkeley school?
items.explanation: Britannica ties U.S. cultural geography to Carl Sauer and the Berkeley school.

items.question: Which famous Indonesian monument appears in UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention page as an example of a major international safeguarding effort?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage Convention narrative highlights restoration work at Borobudur.

items.question: Which site became among the first twelve inscribed on the World Heritage List in 1978?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s convention timeline lists Ecuador’s Galápagos Islands among the first inscriptions in 1978.

items.question: Which strategic objective—the fifth “C”—did the World Heritage Committee add in 2007?
items.explanation: UNESCO’s World Heritage materials describe adding “Communities” as the fifth “C” in 2007.
